directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.
directives.name: include

directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.
directives.name: skip

args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).
args.name: reason

directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.
directives.name: deprecated

args.description: The URL that specifies the behaviour of this scalar.
args.name: url

directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behaviour of this scalar.
directives.name: specifiedBy

types.description: The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.
types.name: String

types.description: The types of events that may be yielded by op and job execution.
types.name: DagsterEventType

types.description: An enumeration.
types.name: ErrorSource

types.description: The `Float` scalar type represents signed double-precision fractional values as specified by [IEEE 754](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IEEE_floating_point).
types.name: Float

types.description: The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.
types.name: Boolean

types.description: The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.
types.name: Int

types.description:
Enumerate the reasons an asset may have failed to materialize. Can be used to provide more granular
    information about the failure to the user.
types.name: AssetMaterializationFailureReason

types.description:
An asset can fail to materialize in two ways: an unexpected/unintentional failure that should update
    the global state of the asset to Failed, and one that indicates that the asset not materializing
    is expected (like an optional asset or a user canceled the run).
types.name: AssetMaterializationFailureType

types.description: The `ID` scalar type represents a unique identifier, often used to refetch an object or as key for a cache. The ID type appears in a JSON response as a String; however, it is not intended to be human-readable. When expected as an input type, any string (such as `"4"`) or integer (such as `4`) input value will be accepted as an ID.
types.name: ID

types.description:
Represents the set of results of the auto-materialize logic.

    MATERIALIZE: The asset should be materialized by a run kicked off on this tick
    SKIP: The asset should not be materialized by a run kicked off on this tick, because future
        ticks are expected to materialize it.
    DISCARD: The asset should not be materialized by a run kicked off on this tick, but future
        ticks are not expected to materialize it.
types.name: AutoMaterializeDecisionType

types.description: An enumeration.
types.name: BackfillPolicyType

types.description:
What change an asset has undergone between two deployments. Used
    in distinguishing asset definition changes in branch deployment and
    in subsequent other deployments.
types.name: ChangeReason

types.description: Enum for the health status of an asset.
types.name: AssetHealthStatus

types.description: An enumeration.
types.name: AutoMaterializePolicyType

types.description:
The primary dimension of a multipartitioned asset is the time-partitioned dimension.
If both dimensions of the asset are static or time-partitioned, the primary dimension is
the first defined dimension.
types.name: MaterializedPartitionRangeStatuses2D

types.description: An enumeration.
types.name: PartitionRangeStatus

types.description: An enumeration.
types.name: StaleStatus

types.description: An enumeration.
types.name: StaleCauseCategory

fields.description: When the check run started
fields.name: timestamp

types.description: An enumeration.
types.name: AssetCheckExecutionResolvedStatus

fields.description: When the check evaluation was stored
fields.name: timestamp

types.description:
Severity level for an AssetCheckResult.

    - WARN: a potential issue with the asset
    - ERROR: a definite issue with the asset

    Severity does not impact execution of the asset or downstream assets.
types.name: AssetCheckSeverity

types.description:
Union type for asset check partition statuses.

The concrete type returned depends on the partitions definition:
- TimeWindowPartitionsDefinition -> AssetCheckTimePartitionStatuses
- MultiPartitionsDefinition -> AssetCheckMultiPartitionStatuses
- Other (Static, Dynamic) -> AssetCheckDefaultPartitionStatuses
types.name: AssetCheckPartitionStatuses

types.description:
Static-partitioned asset check statuses as partition key lists.

Each field contains the list of partition keys with that status.
types.name: AssetCheckDefaultPartitionStatuses

types.description: Multi-partitioned asset check statuses with 2D run-length encoding.
types.name: AssetCheckMultiPartitionStatuses

types.description:
Range in primary dimension with secondary dimension statuses.

The primary dimension is compressed using run-length encoding (consecutive primary
partitions with identical secondary dimension status patterns are grouped).
types.name: AssetCheckMultiPartitionRangeStatuses

types.description: Union for secondary dimension (can be Time or Default).
types.name: AssetCheckPartitionStatus1D

types.description:
Time-partitioned asset check statuses with range compression.

Each range represents a contiguous set of partitions with the same status.
Unlike assets, asset checks expose all 6 statuses as separate ranges.
types.name: AssetCheckTimePartitionStatuses

types.description:
Time range with associated check status.

Uses the same fields as GrapheneTimePartitionRange but with check-specific status.
types.name: AssetCheckTimePartitionRangeStatus

types.description: Status for asset check partition ranges.
types.name: AssetCheckPartitionRangeStatus

types.description:
The `GenericScalar` scalar type represents a generic
GraphQL scalar value that could be:
String, Boolean, Int, Float, List or Object.
types.name: GenericScalar

types.description:
This interface supports the case where we can look up a pipeline successfully in the
repository available to the DagsterInstance/graphql context, as well as the case where we know
that a pipeline exists/existed thanks to materialized data such as logs and run metadata, but
where we can't look the concrete pipeline up.
types.name: PipelineReference

fields.description: Captured logs are the stdout/stderr logs for a given file key within the run
fields.name: capturedLogs

types.description: The status of run execution.

enumValues.description: Runs waiting to be launched by the Dagster Daemon.
enumValues.name: QUEUED

enumValues.description: Runs that have been created, but not yet submitted for launch.
enumValues.name: NOT_STARTED

enumValues.description: Runs that are managed outside of the Dagster control plane.
enumValues.name: MANAGED

enumValues.description: Runs that have been launched, but execution has not yet started.
enumValues.name: STARTING

enumValues.description: Runs that have been launched and execution has started.
enumValues.name: STARTED

enumValues.description: Runs that have successfully completed.
enumValues.name: SUCCESS

enumValues.description: Runs that have failed to complete.
enumValues.name: FAILURE

enumValues.description: Runs that are in-progress and pending to be canceled.
enumValues.name: CANCELING

enumValues.description: Runs that have been canceled before completion.
enumValues.name: CANCELED

types.name: RunStatus

fields.description: Captured logs are the stdout/stderr logs for a given file key within the run
fields.name: capturedLogs

fields.description: Included to comply with RunsFeedEntry interface. Duplicate of status.
fields.name: runStatus

args.description: Truncate the returned selection to at most this many asset keys. Use assetSelectionCount for the untruncated total.
args.name: limit

args.description: Truncate the returned selection to at most this many asset checks. Use assetCheckSelectionCount for the untruncated total.
args.name: limit

fields.description:
This is an odd and problematic field. It recursively goes down to
get all the types contained within a type. The case where it is horrible
are dictionaries and it recurses all the way down to the leaves. This means
that in a case where one is fetching all the types and then all the inner
types keys for those types, we are returning O(N^2) type keys, which
can cause awful performance for large schemas. When you have access
to *all* the types, you should instead only use the type_param_keys
field for closed generic types and manually navigate down the to
field types client-side.

Where it is useful is when you are fetching types independently and
want to be able to render them, but without fetching the entire schema.

We use this capability when rendering the sidebar.
fields.name: recursiveConfigTypes

fields.description:
This returns the keys for type parameters of any closed generic type,
(e.g. List, Optional). This should be used for reconstructing and
navigating the full schema client-side and not innerTypes.
fields.name: typeParamKeys

fields.name: configFields

fields.name: configuredValues

types.description: An enumeration.
types.name: NestedResourceType

types.description: An enumeration.
types.name: DefsStateManagementType

inputFields.description:
The ID of the run at the root of the run group. All partial /
        full re-executions should use the first run as the rootRunID so they are
        grouped together.
inputFields.name: rootRunId

inputFields.description:
The ID of the run serving as the parent within the run group.
        For the first re-execution, this will be the same as the `rootRunId`. For
        subsequent runs, the root or a previous re-execution could be the parent run.
inputFields.name: parentRunId

inputFields.description:
Defines the job / pipeline and solid subset that should be executed.
        All subsequent executions in the same run group (for example, a single-step
        re-execution) are scoped to the original run's selector and solid
        subset.
inputFields.name: selector

inputFields.description:
Defines run tags and parent / root relationships.

Note: To
        'restart from failure', provide a `parentRunId` and pass the
        'dagster/is_resume_retry' tag. Dagster's automatic step key selection will
        override any stepKeys provided.
inputFields.name: executionMetadata

inputFields.description:
Defines step keys to execute within the execution plan defined
        by the pipeline `selector`. To execute the entire execution plan, you can omit
        this parameter, provide an empty array, or provide every step name.
inputFields.name: stepKeys

types.description:
The run config schema represents the all the config type
        information given a certain execution selection and mode of execution of that
        selection. All config interactions (e.g. checking config validity, fetching
        all config types, fetching in a particular config type) should be done
        through this type

fields.description:
Fetch the root environment type. Concretely this is the type that
        is in scope at the root of configuration document for a particular execution selection.
        It is the type that is in scope initially with a blank config editor.
fields.name: rootConfigType

fields.description:
Fetch all the named config types that are in the schema. Useful
        for things like a type browser UI, or for fetching all the types are in the
        scope of a document so that the index can be built for the autocompleting editor.
fields.name: allConfigTypes

fields.description:
Parse a particular run config result. The return value
        either indicates that the validation succeeded by returning
        `PipelineConfigValidationValid` or that there are configuration errors
        by returning `RunConfigValidationInvalid' which containers a list errors
        so that can be rendered for the user
fields.name: isRunConfigValid

fields.description:
The default configuration for this run in yaml. This is
        so that the client does not have to parse JSON client side and assemble
        it into a single yaml document.
fields.name: rootDefaultYaml

types.name: RunConfigSchema

types.description: Contains results from multiple pipeline launches.
types.name: LaunchMultipleRunsResult

types.description:
This type is used when passing in a configuration object
        for pipeline configuration. Can either be passed in as a string (the
        YAML configuration object) or as the configuration object itself. In
        either case, the object must conform to the constraints of the dagster config type system.
types.name: RunConfigData

types.name: ResetSensorMutation

A run tag and the free-form values that have been associated
        with it so far.
types.name: PipelineTagAndValues

types.description: An invocation of a solid within a repo.
types.name: NodeInvocationSite

types.description: A solid definition and its invocations within the repo.
types.name: UsedSolid

types.description:
A single app-managed component, persisted in defs state storage.

``attributes`` is the YAML source for the component's attributes block,
stored verbatim so the editing UI can round-trip user input.
types.name: AppManagedComponent

types.description: A JSON Schema document, returned as a parsed JSON object.
types.name: JsonSchema

types.description:
A component's JSON Schema pre-split server-side for react-jsonschema-form.

The raw ``schema`` carries Dagster-specific conventions (inline ``ui:*``
hints, unset-default sentinels, Jinja string escape-hatch variants). Rather
than have the frontend reverse-engineer those, the server splits the schema
once via ``dagster.components.resolved.form_schema.split_form_schema`` and
exposes the resulting RJSF-ready pair here.

fields.description: The cleaned JSON Schema RJSF validates and renders against.
fields.name: dataSchema

fields.description: The parallel RJSF uiSchema holding lifted ``ui:*`` hints.
fields.name: uiSchema

types.name: ComponentFormSchema

types.description: Metadata for a single Component class registered in a code location.

fields.description: The plugin namespace this component belongs to (e.g. ``dagster_test``).
fields.name: namespace

fields.description: Sample YAML rendered server-side from the component's JSON Schema. Used to seed the docs ``Example`` panel.
fields.name: example

fields.description: The JSON Schema describing the component's attributes model. May be null if the component does not declare a model.
fields.name: schema

fields.description: The ``schema`` split server-side into the (dataSchema, uiSchema) pair react-jsonschema-form expects. Null when the component declares no model.
fields.name: formSchema

fields.description: Whether instances of this component class may be created and edited from the UI via the app-managed components workflow. Today every component class is editable; in the future this will be an opt-in hook on the Component class so library authors can mark their components app-managed explicitly.
fields.name: isAppManaged

types.description:
The account review status of an Organization.
    New GA users are automatically added as "LEAD".
    When they convert from trial they become "CUSTOMER"
    Some customers with high fraud scores may be challenged with 2fa
    or a credit card challenge. If they fail they will enter "PENDING_REVIEW".
    If they fail manual review they will enter "REJECTED" and lose access
    to their serverless trial.
types.name: DagsterCloudAccountReviewStatus

types.description:
The category of Plan an Organization is signed up for. Must match the
    plan_details tuple set in DagsterCloudPlan.
    New GA users are automatically entered into a STANDARD plan.
    ENTERPRISE plans are negotiated individually with larger customers,
    these have bespoke contracts.
    We use OPPORTUNITY to ungate enterprise features during negotiations
    but before a contract has been signed.

types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.
fields.name: types

fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.
fields.name: queryType

fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.
fields.name: mutationType

fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.
fields.name: subscriptionType

fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.
fields.name: directives

types.name: __Schema

types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.
types.name: __Type

types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.
enumValues.name: SCALAR

enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.
enumValues.name: OBJECT

enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.
enumValues.name: INTERFACE

enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.
enumValues.name: UNION

enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.
enumValues.name: ENUM

enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.
enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT

enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.
enumValues.name: LIST

enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.
enumValues.name: NON_NULL

types.name: __TypeKind

types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.
types.name: __Field

types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.
fields.name: defaultValue

types.name: __InputValue

types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.
types.name: __EnumValue

types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.
types.name: __Directive

types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.
enumValues.name: QUERY

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.
enumValues.name: MUTATION

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.
enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.
enumValues.name: FIELD

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.
enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.
enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.
enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.
enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
